# Add a server-stamped ingestedAt timestamp to tenant KB chunk metadata

## Context

A recurring substrate gap surfaced across three Phase 4 / cloud-KB features: **tenant KB chunks carry no ingest timestamp.** A stamped chunk's metadata (`VectorService.resolveTenantStamp` → `applyTenantStamp`) is `{tenantId, repoSlug, visibility, tenantConfigVersion, originAgentIdentity, ...parsed-chunk-v1 fields}` — there is no `ingestedAt` / `createdAt`. `parsed-chunk-v1.schema.json` has no timestamp field either.

Three features have now hit this gap:
- **#11640** (Phase 4B reconciliation daemon) — wanted a wall-clock orphan-grace window; had to use a `tenantConfigVersion` version-gap instead because no per-chunk timestamp exists.
- **#11711** (reconciliation V1.x) — manifest-grace reasoning inherits the same gap.
- **#11641** (Phase 4C GC daemon) — **blocked**: time-based + count-based retention policies ("retain last 90 days" / "retain last N chunks") have no substrate without a per-chunk timestamp; version-based-only retention would merely duplicate #11640.

Three independent consumers wanting the same one-field stamp is a clear substrate insufficiency.

## The Problem

Retention, age-based GC, and time-windowed reconciliation all need to answer "when was this chunk ingested?" Today that question is unanswerable from chunk metadata. Each consuming feature has had to work around it (version-gap proxies) or defer scope. The fix is a single additive field, stamped server-side at embed time.

## The Architectural Reality

- `ai/services/knowledge-base/VectorService.mjs` — `resolveTenantStamp(tenantContext)` builds the server-derived tenant stamp; `applyTenantStamp(chunk, stamp)` spreads it onto the chunk (`{...chunk, ...stamp, hash, id}`). The stamp is the natural home for `ingestedAt`.
- `TENANT_GUARDED_FIELDS` (`VectorService.mjs:13`) — the server-derived fields validated against client spoofing. `ingestedAt` is purely server-derived → belongs in this list.
- `createTenantAwareChunkId` hashes `{tenantId, repoSlug, hash, type, name, source}` — it does **not** include the stamp's volatile fields, so adding `ingestedAt` does not perturb chunk IDs (a same-content re-push resolves to the same ID).

## The Fix

Add `ingestedAt` (epoch ms, `Date.now()` at stamp time) to `resolveTenantStamp`'s output and to `TENANT_GUARDED_FIELDS`. It then flows through `applyTenantStamp` onto every chunk's Chroma metadata, queryable by downstream retention / GC / reconciliation consumers.

**Semantics — resolved (PR #11713 Cycle-1, @neo-gpt review):** `ingestedAt` marks when a chunk row is **actually embedded / upserted**. `embed()`'s zero-change fast path dedupes already-known content-hash IDs, so a *same-content* re-push does not re-upsert and the chunk keeps its prior `ingestedAt`; a *content change* yields a new content-hash chunk row with its own fresh `ingestedAt`. This embed-time anchor (≈ "first-embedded-at for this content") is the correct retention semantics — re-pushing identical content must not reset its age.

## Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `VectorService.resolveTenantStamp` → chunk metadata `ingestedAt` | this ticket; the #11641 / #11640 / #11711 consumers | `resolveTenantStamp` adds `ingestedAt: Date.now()` (epoch ms); `applyTenantStamp` spreads it onto every embedded chunk's metadata. It marks the actual embed/upsert — an unchanged re-push (zero-change fast path) retains the prior value. | A chunk embedded *before* this ticket has no `ingestedAt` — consumers MUST treat a missing `ingestedAt` as "unknown age" and fail-safe (never expire / action a chunk with no timestamp), mirroring #11640's missing-`tenantConfigVersion` skip. | Yes — JSDoc | Unit: `resolveTenantStamp` includes a finite `ingestedAt`; `applyTenantStamp` propagates it; a same-content re-push retains it |
| `TENANT_GUARDED_FIELDS` | #11631 write-side tenant stamping | `ingestedAt` is added — a client-supplied `ingestedAt` is rejected / overwritten by the server value (it is server-derived, never client-authored). | Per the existing `spoofRejectionMode` policy. | Yes — JSDoc | Unit: a client-supplied `ingestedAt` is overwritten with the server value |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `resolveTenantStamp` stamps `ingestedAt` (epoch ms) on the tenant stamp.
- [ ] `ingestedAt` propagates onto embedded chunk metadata via `applyTenantStamp`.
- [ ] `ingestedAt` is in `TENANT_GUARDED_FIELDS` (server-derived; client-spoof-rejected).
- [ ] Re-push semantics documented — `ingestedAt` is stamped at the actual embed / upsert; an unchanged same-content re-push (the zero-change fast path) retains the prior value.
- [ ] Unit tests: stamp inclusion, propagation, guarded-field spoof rejection, same-content-re-push retention.

## Out of Scope

- Consuming `ingestedAt` for retention / GC — that is #11641 (which this ticket unblocks).
- Backfilling `ingestedAt` onto chunks embedded before this ticket — consumers fail-safe on a missing value; a backfill (if ever needed) is a separate concern.

## Related

- [#11641](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11641) — Phase 4C GC daemon (**blocked by this ticket** — time/count-based retention needs `ingestedAt`).
- [#11640](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11640) / [#11711](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11711) — reconciliation; both worked around the absent timestamp via a version-gap.
- [#11631](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11631) — write-side tenant stamping (the `TENANT_GUARDED_FIELDS` precedent).
- [#11628](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/11628) — Phase 4 epic (this is a Phase-2-substrate enabler for it).

- 2026-05-21T08:14:01Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `1810a34` - "fix(ai): correct ingestedAt re-push semantics — embed-time, not last-push (#11712)

Addresses @neo-gpt's PR #11713 Cycle-1 Required Action. Commit 659a92700's
JSDoc + the PR/ticket prose claimed a same-content re-push "re-embeds +
re-stamps ingestedAt — last-ingested-at semantics." That is wrong: embed()'s
zero-change fast path dedupes already-known content-hash IDs out of
chunksToProcess, so an unchanged re-push does not upsert and does not refresh
ingestedAt.

- VectorService.mjs — resolveTenantStamp JSDoc corrected: ingestedAt marks when
  the chunk row is actually embedded/upserted; a same-content re-push retains
  its prior ingestedAt (a content change yields a new content-hash chunk row
  with its own fresh ingestedAt).
- VectorService.tenantStamping.spec.mjs — +1 test proving the corrected
  contract: a byte-identical re-push leaves spy.calls.upsert at 1 and the row's
  ingestedAt unchanged.

No production-logic change — resolveTenantStamp still stamps ingestedAt at
embed time; only the documented semantic boundary is corrected. The embed-time
("first-embedded-at for this content") semantics is the correct retention
anchor anyway — re-pushing identical content should not reset its age.

11/11 VectorService.tenantStamping.spec.mjs tests pass; node --check clean.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>"
- 2026-05-21T09:33:38Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d03179a` - "feat(ai): stamp ingestedAt on tenant KB chunks (#11712) (#11713)

* feat(ai): stamp ingestedAt on tenant KB chunks (#11712)

Tenant KB chunks carried no ingest timestamp — a substrate gap that blocked
#11641's time/count-based retention, #11640's wall-clock orphan-grace, and
#11711's manifest-grace. This adds the one missing field.

- ai/services/knowledge-base/VectorService.mjs — resolveTenantStamp now stamps
  ingestedAt (epoch ms, Date.now() at embed time) onto the server-derived
  tenant stamp; it flows via applyTenantStamp onto every embedded chunk's
  Chroma metadata. ingestedAt joins TENANT_GUARDED_FIELDS — purely
  server-derived, so a client-supplied ingestedAt is overwritten (or rejected
  under spoofRejectionMode: 'reject'), consistent with tenantConfigVersion.
  Re-push re-embeds + re-stamps it: last-ingested-at semantics.
- VectorService.tenantStamping.spec.mjs — 3 tests: server-stamp inclusion +
  propagation onto chunk metadata, client-spoof overwrite + diagnostics,
  reject-mode rejection before any Chroma write.

Consumers fail-safe on a missing ingestedAt (a chunk embedded before #11712) —
unknown-age, never expire/action it (mirrors #11640's missing-stamp skip).

10/10 VectorService.tenantStamping.spec.mjs tests pass; node --check clean.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

- 2026-05-21T10:31:54Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #11714
- 2026-05-21T11:19:32Z @tobiu referenced in commit `82ea006` - "feat(ai): KB garbage-collection daemon — Phase 4C (#11641) (#11715)

* feat(ai): add KB garbage-collection retention engine (#11641)

Phase 4C (#11641) substrate — the pure retention-expiry classification core
of the forthcoming KB garbage-collection daemon (KbGarbageCollectionService).

ai/services/knowledge-base/helpers/KbGarbageCollectionEngine.mjs —
dependency-free (no Neo, no I/O, no clock):
- selectExpiredChunks — classifies a tenant's Chroma rows under OR-expiry: a
  chunk is expired if time-expired (now - ingestedAt > maxAgeMs) OR
  count-expired (beyond the maxCount most-recent of its {tenantId, repoSlug}
  bucket, ranked ingestedAt desc then chunk id asc — a deterministic
  tie-breaker for the batch-shared ingestedAt case). Built on the #11712
  ingestedAt chunk stamp.
- resolveRetention — normalizes the {maxAgeMs, maxCount} operator policy
  (each dimension independently degrades to null/disabled when invalid).
- formatGcDetail — pure Phase 4A telemetry detail payload.

Fail-safe: a chunk with a missing/non-numeric ingestedAt (a pre-#11712
ingest) is never flagged — unaged + unrankable, excluded from both expiry
paths (mirrors #11640's missing-tenantConfigVersion skip).

Per the #11641 Contract Ledger (ticket body, T3 — refined per @neo-gpt's
pre-branch peer review): the explicit OR-expiry, the deterministic count
tie-breaker, and {tenantId, repoSlug} count-bucketing are engine contract.
The daemon (poll loop + Chroma I/O + opt-in delete + the defrag-recommended
signal) lands in follow-up commits.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* feat(ai): add KB garbage-collection daemon (#11641)

Phase 4C (#11641) — the KB garbage-collection daemon: periodically evaluates
each tenant's persisted Chroma chunks against an operator retention policy and
(opt-in) deletes the retention-expired ones. Builds on the retention engine
from 03974a30f.

- ai/daemons/KbGarbageCollectionService.mjs — the poll-loop daemon (Neo
  singleton, the KbReconciliationService pattern: start/stop/scheduleNext/
  pulse). Each pulse enumerates tenants (distinct tenantId from
  getTenantIngestionRollup), runs the pure KbGarbageCollectionEngine against
  each tenant's where:{tenantId} Chroma rows, and — when gcAutoDelete is set —
  deletes the expired chunks; a two-pass pulse then stamps the post-loop
  defragRecommended fact onto each tenant's 'tombstone' telemetry and emits a
  defrag-recommended logger.warn when cumulative deletion exceeds
  gcDefragThreshold. All I/O behind test-stubbable seam methods; pulse() is
  try/catch/finally + per-tenant try/catch.
- ai/scripts/kb-gc-daemon.mjs — the thin Neo-bootstrap wrapper.
- ai/config.template.mjs — 5 new aiConfig.knowledgeBase keys (gcEnabled /
  gcIntervalMs / gcRetention / gcAutoDelete / gcDefragThreshold), read
  defensively.
- package.json — the ai:kb-gc script.

Per the #11641 Contract Ledger (ticket body, T3 — refined per @neo-gpt's
pre-branch peer review): V1 is purely additive (touches no merged Phase 2
code); the daemon is opt-in (gcEnabled=false default), the destructive delete
a second opt-in (gcAutoDelete=false default), and V1 emits a defrag-recommended
signal rather than spawning ai:defrag-kb (the spawn + its concurrency story
are V1.x).

node --check clean on both .mjs files; config.template.mjs valid; package.json
valid JSON. The 2 unit specs land in a follow-up commit.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* test(ai): KB GC engine + daemon specs (#11641)

Phase 4C (#11641) — unit coverage for the KB garbage-collection daemon: 17
pure engine tests + 18 daemon tests, 35 passing.

- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/knowledge-base/KbGarbageCollectionEngine.spec.mjs
  — covers selectExpiredChunks (time-expiry strict-greater-than, count-expiry
  per {tenantId,repoSlug} bucket, the deterministic id-asc tie-break on equal
  ingestedAt, the OR-union, the missing-ingestedAt fail-safe, the empty-policy
  no-op), resolveRetention (per-dimension validation), formatGcDetail. Pure
  module -> no setup() harness.
- test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/KbGarbageCollectionService.spec.mjs — covers
  start/stop (opt-in gate, idempotency, interval config), pulse (empty-tenant
  early return, per-tenant loop, reschedule-on-failure, null-result isolation,
  the defrag-recommended threshold signal both ways), collectTenant
  (clean-tenant no-delete, auto-delete opt-in gating both ways, never-throws),
  fetchTenants dedup, deleteChunks empty-guard, the recordGcMetric tombstone
  payload. Seam-stubbing on the singleton per the KbReconciliationService.spec
  precedent.

35/35 passing (npm run test-unit). Satisfies the #11641 Contract Ledger
Evidence columns.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Opus 4.7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>
